questions.question_text: What is the common difference of the arithmetic sequence 3, 7, 11, 15, 19?
questions.explanation: Common difference = 7 - 3 = 4. Each term increases by 4.

questions.question_text: The first term of an arithmetic sequence is 5 and the common difference is 3. What is the 10th term?
Write the explicit formula for the arithmetic sequence: 2, 9, 16, 23, ...
What is the 20th term of the arithmetic sequence where a(1) = -4 and d = 6?
Is the sequence 5, 10, 20, 40, 80 arithmetic? Why or why not?

options.text: No, the differences between consecutive terms are not constant (5, 10, 20, 40)
No, because the sequence has negative numbers

questions.explanation: The differences are 5, 10, 20, 40, which are not constant. This is a geometric sequence (common ratio 2), not an arithmetic sequence.

questions.question_text: The 5th term of an arithmetic sequence is 23 and the common difference is 4. What is the first term?
What is the recursive formula for an arithmetic sequence with first term 10 and common difference -3?
questions.explanation: A recursive formula defines each term from the previous one. With a(1) = 10 and d = -3: a(n) = a(n-1) + (-3) = a(n-1) - 3.

questions.question_text: The arithmetic sequence a(n) = 4n + 1 defines terms for n = 1, 2, 3, .... What are the first four terms?
An arithmetic sequence has a(3) = 14 and a(7) = 30. What is the common difference?
questions.explanation: From term 3 to term 7 is 4 steps. Change = 30 - 14 = 16. Common difference = 16 / 4 = 4.

questions.question_text: What is a(50) for the arithmetic sequence a(n) = -2n + 100?
What is an arithmetic sequence?

options.text: A sequence where each term is multiplied by a constant to get the next term
A sequence where a constant value (common difference) is added to each term to get the next term
A sequence that always starts at zero

questions.explanation: An arithmetic sequence has a constant difference between consecutive terms. Each term equals the previous term plus the common difference.

questions.question_text: What is the 'common difference' in an arithmetic sequence?

options.text: The constant amount added to each term to produce the next term
The first term of the sequence

questions.explanation: The common difference (d) is the constant value that is added to each term to get the next term. It can be found by subtracting any term from the next term.

questions.question_text: What is an 'explicit formula' for a sequence?

options.text: A formula that defines each term based on the previous term
A formula that directly calculates any term from its position number n
A formula that only works for the first term
A formula that lists all terms of the sequence

questions.explanation: An explicit formula calculates any term directly from its position number n without needing to know the previous terms. For arithmetic sequences: a(n) = a(1) + (n-1)d.

questions.question_text: What is a 'recursive formula' for a sequence?

options.text: A formula that calculates any term directly from n
A formula that defines each term in relation to the previous term(s), along with the first term
A formula that only works for geometric sequences
A formula that gives the sum of all terms

questions.explanation: A recursive formula defines each term using the previous term. For arithmetic sequences: a(n) = a(n-1) + d, with a specified first term.

questions.question_text: What does 'nth term' mean?

options.text: The first term of a sequence
The term at position number n in a sequence
The last term of a sequence

questions.explanation: The nth term is the term located at position n in the sequence. For example, the 5th term is at position n = 5.

questions.question_text: What does 'linear function' have to do with arithmetic sequences?

options.text: Arithmetic sequences have no connection to linear functions
An arithmetic sequence can be represented as a linear function where the common difference is the slope
Linear functions only produce geometric sequences

questions.explanation: An arithmetic sequence with common difference d and first term a(1) can be written as a(n) = dn + (a(1) - d), which is a linear function of n with slope d.

questions.question_text: What does 'term' mean in a sequence?

options.text: An individual value or element in the sequence

questions.explanation: A term is a single value in the sequence. For example, in 3, 7, 11, 15, the first term is 3 and the third term is 11.

questions.question_text: What does 'first term' (a(1)) represent in an arithmetic sequence?

options.text: The starting value of the sequence

questions.explanation: The first term a(1) is the starting value of the sequence from which all other terms are generated by repeatedly adding the common difference.

questions.question_text: What does 'position number' mean in a sequence?

options.text: The value of the term itself
The index (n) that identifies where a term falls in the sequence (1st, 2nd, 3rd, etc.)
The last term in the sequence

questions.explanation: The position number (n) tells you which term you are looking at: n = 1 for the first term, n = 2 for the second, and so on.

questions.question_text: What does 'constant rate of change' mean in the context of arithmetic sequences?

options.text: The terms of the sequence are multiplied by the same number each time
The same amount (common difference) is added to get from one term to the next
The terms alternate between positive and negative

questions.explanation: A constant rate of change means the same amount is added each time, which is the defining property of an arithmetic sequence.

questions.question_text: A gym membership costs $50 to join and $30 per month. Write the total cost as an arithmetic sequence where a(n) is the total cost after n months. What is a(12)?
A theater has 20 seats in the first row and adds 4 seats in each subsequent row. How many seats are in the 15th row?
A student saves money each week. After week 1, she has $25. Each week she adds $15. Write an explicit formula and find how much she has saved after 10 weeks.
A staircase is being built. The first step is 8 inches above the ground, and each additional step adds 8 inches. Which step first reaches or exceeds 6 feet (72 inches)?
questions.explanation: a(n) = 8n. Set 8n >= 72: n >= 9. The 9th step reaches exactly 72 inches (6 feet).

questions.question_text: A taxi driver notices that on Monday he drove 40 miles, Tuesday 55 miles, Wednesday 70 miles, and so on. If this arithmetic pattern continues, how many miles will he drive on Saturday (day 6)?
A concert venue sells tickets starting at $50 for row 1. Each row farther from the stage costs $5 less. What is the ticket price for row 8?
An employee earns $35,000 in year 1 and receives a $2,000 raise each year. What is the salary in year 10, and what type of function models this?
questions.explanation: a(n) = 35000 + (n-1)(2000). a(10) = 35000 + 9(2000) = 35000 + 18000 = $53,000. The constant increase makes this a linear function.

questions.question_text: A farmer plants 100 trees in year 1 and plants 25 additional trees each year. After how many years will the farmer have planted at least 500 trees total?
questions.explanation: Trees planted in year n: a(n) = 100 + (n-1)(25) = 75 + 25n. Set 75 + 25n >= 500: 25n >= 425, n >= 17. After 17 years.

questions.question_text: A stack of boxes has 1 box on top, 4 boxes in the next layer, 7 in the next, and so on. How many boxes are in the 8th layer?
A phone plan charges a one-time activation fee of $20 and $45 per month. Represent the total cost as an arithmetic sequence. In what month will the total cost first exceed $500?
questions.explanation: Total cost after n months: a(n) = 20 + 45n. Set 20 + 45n > 500: 45n > 480, n > 10.67. Since n must be a whole number, the total first exceeds $500 in month 11: a(11) = 20 + 495 = $515.
